name: WV590 Hot-Spots — booking + google-flights
description: Close the 42 fails on the two dominant capability hot-spots from the 2026-04-28 WebVoyager-590 baseline (90.8%). Date-picker dialog-stuck pattern accounts for 30+ fails across both sites. Goal: research architectural changes that generalize across sites, not site-specific overfits.

perSite.8 sites at 100%: allrecipes amazon apple arxiv bbc-news cambridge coursera google-map

hypotheses.name: Calendar month-navigation macro for date pickers
hypotheses.rationale: 27/27 google-flights fails involve the date-picker dialog. ~10/15 booking fails involve the 'calendar only shows next 2 months' pattern — agent has to click 'next month' iteratively to reach Dec 2026, burning 6-8 turns per click sequence. Both sites use a similar pattern: open dialog → calendar shows current month → user must navigate to target month. A reusable macro 'navigateCalendarToMonth(year, month)' that detects month-name elements and clicks 'next' until target is visible would condense 6-8 turns into 1 macro call. Generalizable to ANY site with month-navigation calendars (Airbnb, Expedia, etc).
hypotheses.risk: Macro selector may be fragile across calendar implementations. Mitigation: detect month-name pattern via regex on visible text, fall back to manual navigation if not found.

treatment._note: New macro in src/macros/ + register in skills/macros/. Detection: dialog containing month-name text. Action: click 'next month' button N times until target visible, click target date.
treatment.scope: all sites that present month-navigation calendars

hypotheses.name: URL-direct construction when goal specifies all parameters
hypotheses.rationale: Google Flights URL `?tfs=...` carries origin+dest+date+passengers. Booking URL `?ss=Paris&checkin=2026-12-01&checkout=2026-12-06&group_adults=2` does the same. When the goal specifies all required params (origin/destination/dates/count) the agent could construct the URL directly and skip the dialog entirely. Currently the brain only does navigate(URL) for the homepage. Adding a 'try URL-direct first, fall back to UI flow on failure' strategy bypasses the date-picker bottleneck completely.
hypotheses.expected_impact: pass rate: +3-5pp overall; concentrated on flights/booking/airbnb-style sites
hypotheses.risk: Hand-coded URL templates per site is overfitting. Mitigation: ship as a 'site profile' system (declarative URL templates) rather than hardcoded conditionals; require goal-extracted params to all be present before attempting; on any non-200 or redirect-to-homepage, fall back to UI flow.

treatment._note: New src/site-profiles/ with URL templates for top-failure sites + brain integration. Profile shape: {domain, urlTemplate, requiredParams, paramExtractors}.

hypotheses.name: Dialog-aware snapshot focusing
hypotheses.rationale: When a modal dialog is open (date picker, occupancy selector), the snapshot still includes the entire underlying page DOM. This dilutes attention and inflates context — booking/flights dialog turns hit 200-300k tokens with vision on. Detecting an open dialog (role=dialog, aria-modal=true, or visible-blocker pattern) and FILTERING the snapshot to just the dialog subtree forces the agent to focus on the immediate task. Token cost drops + decision quality improves.
hypotheses.expected_impact: pass rate: +1-3pp overall; cost: -10-15% on dialog-heavy sites
hypotheses.risk: Could miss state outside the dialog (toast notifications, page errors). Mitigation: keep a small 'page-context' header (URL, page title, top-level nav state) above the dialog snapshot.

treatment._note: Modify src/observe/ to detect [role=dialog], [aria-modal=true], and z-index>1000 patterns; when detected, scope the DOM snapshot to dialog subtree + a 200-char page header.
treatment.scope: all sites — improves dialog interaction broadly

hypotheses.name: State-regression detection + URL-recovery
hypotheses.rationale: Booking specifically has a 'redirect to homepage with errorc_searchstring_not_found' pattern that wipes search context. The agent currently treats this as 'dom changed slightly' and continues — but the search state is gone. Detect 'URL changed unexpectedly to root domain' or 'expected entity missing from page' as a state-regression signal and trigger recovery (re-navigate to known-good URL with search params, or restart the search flow).
hypotheses.expected_impact: pass rate: +2-3pp overall (closes booking search-reset cluster)
hypotheses.risk: False positives on legitimate redirects (e.g., login walls). Mitigation: only trigger when the regression is to root path AND the agent had progressed past root in a prior turn.

treatment._note: Add to src/runner/recovery.ts: track URL progression, detect 'we were on /search/results then we got bounced to /', emit recovery event with 'state regressed; re-navigate to last-known-good URL'.

hypotheses.name: Adaptive max-turns based on detected flow complexity
hypotheses.rationale: 21 fails are 'agent_gave_up_at_max_turns'. Current default is max_turns=15 per case across all sites. Booking flows are 12-20 turns (date pickers + filters + result extraction); single-page lookups are 3-5 turns. Adaptive sizing — start with 15, allow extension to 25 if the agent has shown verifiable progress in the last 3 turns (URL change AND DOM significantly different) — prevents premature aborts on hard cases without rewarding spinning agents.
hypotheses.risk: Could waste budget on cases that genuinely can't succeed. Mitigation: extension only granted on verified progress signal; cap at 25 absolute.

treatment._note: src/runner/runner.ts — track lastProgressTurn; when hitting maxTurns, if lastProgressTurn was within 3 turns ago, grant +5 extension up to 25 total.

hypotheses.name: Vision-only mode on SPA detection
hypotheses.rationale: Both booking and flights are React-heavy SPAs where the DOM snapshot is voluminous and unstable but the visual layout is consistent. For these specific sites, vision is more reliable than DOM-vision-hybrid. Detect SPA patterns (heavy use of data-* attributes, React/Vue runtime markers, lots of generated class names) and switch to vision-only observation. Reduces context size + makes the agent reason about what it sees, not what's in the DOM.
hypotheses.expected_impact: pass rate: +1-3pp on flights/booking; cost: -20% on SPA-heavy
hypotheses.risk: Vision is less reliable for hidden/off-screen elements. Mitigation: only activate when DOM-snapshot-bytes > threshold AND turns-without-progress >= 2.

treatment._note: src/observe/ — detect SPA via DOM heuristics; add vision-only fallback path when activation conditions met.

hypotheses.name: Domain-aware cost cap (300k → 500k for vision-heavy)
hypotheses.rationale: 9 fails are cost_cap_exceeded. 4 of them are on flights/booking/espn where pages are inherently large × vision-on context. Raising the cap to 500k for known vision-heavy domains trades ~$0.05 extra per case for closing 4-9 fails. Lowest-leverage hypothesis but cheap.
hypotheses.expected_impact: pass rate: +1-2pp; cost: +5% on listed domains, 0% elsewhere
hypotheses.risk: Encourages runaway loops to burn more before the cap fires. Mitigation: pair with state-regression detection (#4) so loops are caught earlier.

treatment._note: src/run-state.ts — domain-aware DEFAULT_TOKEN_BUDGET lookup table; default 300k, override 500k for {booking.com, google.com/travel/flights, espn.com}.
